data.title: Intesa Sanpaolo: Discounted Valuation With Sector-Leading Returns and Above-Consensus Growth Justify Buy Rating
data.description:
In a report released on March 26, Sofie Peterzens from Goldman Sachs upgraded Intesa Sanpaolo SpA to a Buy, with a price target of €6.90.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sofie Peterzens has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight both valuation appeal and earnings momentum. After lagging the wider European bank index, Intesa Sanpaolo now trades at a modest discount to peers on forward earnings multiples, while still offering superior profitability, which in Peterzens’s view sets up room for a valuation catch‑up.

Peterzens also anticipates resilient growth in core revenues, supported by rising net interest income from the replicating portfolio and expanding fee income from wealth and protection activities. Combined with ongoing cost efficiency gains, sector‑leading returns above 20% through 2028, and a double‑digit capital return yield, these drivers underpin forecasts that sit above market consensus and justify the upgraded Buy stance.

According to TipRanks, Peterzens is an analyst with an average return of -7.2% and a 33.73% success rate. Peterzens covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Banco Santander, UniCredit SpA, and Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria.
